title: Immunities & Privileges
lead: Privileges are enjoyed by the House collectively, its committees and members individually — without which they cannot discharge their functions. In [[Sita Soren v. UoI (2024)]], a ==7-judge bench unanimously overruled [[P.V. Narasimha Rao (1998)]]==: Arts 105(2)/194(2) give ==no immunity for bribery==.

items: Protect lawmakers from ==interference== in discharging essential functions.
Differential treatment justified by their duties as a ==particular class of public officials==.
Speaking and voting ==without fear of intimidation== is intrinsic to representation.
A breach of privilege is also ==contempt of the House== — the reverse is not true.

head: Case
Holding
rows: ==Art 194(3) > Art 19(1)(a)== — special protection prevails over the general right.
[[Dr Jatish Chandra Ghosh v. Hari Sadhan (1961)]]
Acting in a ==private capacity== does not invoke Art 194.
Privileges are ==not absolute== — subject to judicial review; cannot violate fundamental rights.
==Two-fold test==: the act must relate to the ==collective functions of the House== AND bear a ==‘functional relationship’== to the legislator's duty — bribery fails both.

items.title: Issues
bullets: Conflict with ==free speech and other FRs==

items.title: Solutions
bullets: Cross-party dialogue; respect for rights and procedure
==Rule 380==: Speaker may expunge unparliamentary words — but ==only the offending word==, not sentences or paragraphs

head: Conduct
Appropriate consequence
rows: Party accountability; ordinarily ==not loss of seat==.
==Vote exchanged for money, office or benefit==
Criminal prosecution, electoral disqualification, possible ==annulment of election==.
Coordinated defection showing abandonment of membership
‘==Voluntarily giving up membership==’ proceedings, with due process.

blocks.text: ==Cross-voting should not automatically be equated with corruption.==

items: ==Time-bound investigation== of alleged bribery in elections
Special prosecutorial teams where ==prima facie evidence== exists
==Whistle-blower protection== for MLAs; mandatory reporting of attempted inducements
Fast-track courts; ==interim preservation== of electronic, financial and travel records
Disqualification on conviction; explicit ==ECI power to countermand== a materially compromised poll, subject to prompt judicial review

blocks.text: Parliamentary privileges are not ornamental relics of a bygone constitutional era — they are not individual entitlements but institutional guarantees. In a constitutional democracy governed by the rule of law, privileges must serve democracy, not dominate it.
